name: Ala Moana Beach Park (Magic Island Reef)
tagline: An urban reef snorkel that Honolulu locals hit on their lunch break.
parking: Large free lot at Ala Moana Beach Park; metered spots along the peninsula
hours: Park open 4am–10pm; gates lock at night
bestTime: Morning before trade winds chop up the surface; summer months for clearest water
talkStory: Most people come to Ala Moana to sunbathe but the reef along the Magic Island side has surprisingly good snorkeling. Swim out past the sandy bottom and you hit coral with parrotfish, wrasse, and the occasional turtle. The lagoon side is super calm and great for kids just getting comfortable with a mask. The park has showers, restrooms, and is right next to Ala Moana Center if you need food or gear. One of the most convenient snorkel spots on the island.
